question: [File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take / settlementFee] Can an unprivileged taker, by choosing units=1 at a tick where tickToPrice returns a price very close to the settlement fee, cause buyerAssets - sellerAssets == 0 (emitted in EventsLib.Take as buyerAssets and sellerAssets), so claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] receives zero increment despite a real trade, under preconditions: market with max settlementFeeCbp at 0d breakpoint, units=1, offerPrice near settlementFee, triggering the path claimableSettlementFee[offer.market.loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets, violating invariant: every take must accrue nonzero settlement fee when settlementFee > 0, causing scoped impact: systematic fee leakage to zero for dust-unit trades? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, CBP/WAD] and assert buyerAssets - sellerAssets > 0 whenever settlementFee(id, ttm) > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: settlementFee] Can an unprivileged market creator or taker exploit the integer truncation in (feeLower * (end - timeToMaturity) + feeUpper * (timeToMaturity - start)) / (end - start) to obtain a settlement fee that is systematically lower than the true piecewise-linear value, under preconditions: feeLower and feeUpper set to max CBP values, timeToMaturity chosen to maximize truncation error (e.g., timeToMaturity = start + 1), violating invariant: emitted sellerAssets and buyerAssets must reflect the correct fee spread, causing scoped impact: extractable value via repeated takes at fee-minimizing TTM values? Proof idea: differential test settlementFee(id, ttm) against a reference rational arithmetic implementation for all TTM values in [0, 360 days] and assert error <= 1 wei.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the asymmetric rounding of buyerAssets and sellerAssets (mulDivDown for buy offers, mulDivUp for sell offers) to receive more sellerAssets than they pay in buyerAssets net of fees, under preconditions: sell offer (offer.buy=false), units chosen so mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) - mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) < actual settlementFee, violating invariant: claimableSettlementFee must increase by exactly buyerAssets - sellerAssets >= 0 on every take, causing scoped impact: protocol fee undercollection or negative fee accumulation? Proof idea: fuzz units and tick for sell offers and assert buyerAssets >= sellerAssets always.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take / updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged buyer exploit mulDivDown rounding in buyerPendingFeeIncrease = buyerCreditIncrease.mulDivDown(continuousFee * timeToMaturity, WAD) to accumulate credit with systematically zero pendingFee when continuousFee * timeToMaturity < WAD / buyerCreditIncrease, under preconditions: market with nonzero continuousFee, small buyerCreditIncrease (e.g., 1 unit), timeToMaturity near zero, violating invariant: every credit increase must create proportional pendingFee obligation, causing scoped impact: lender receives credit with no fee obligation, leaking continuous fee revenue? Proof idea: fuzz buyerCreditIncrease in [1, WAD/continuousFee] and assert pendingFeeIncrease > 0 whenever continuousFee > 0 and timeToMaturity > 0.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker trigger an overflow in continuousFee * timeToMaturity before division by WAD in buyerPendingFeeIncrease computation, under preconditions: continuousFee = MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE = uint32(0.01e18 / 365 days) ≈ 317, timeToMaturity = 100 * 365 days ≈ 3.15e9 seconds, product ≈ 317 * 3.15e9 ≈ 9.98e11 which fits in uint256 but fuzz with extreme values, violating invariant: buyerPendingFeeIncrease must not overflow uint128, causing scoped impact: silent truncation of pending fee obligation or revert? Proof idea: unit test with continuousFee=MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE and timeToMaturity=100*365 days and assert toUint128 does not revert.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: updatePositionView / _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged lender exploit mulDivDown rounding in postSlashCredit = credit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - lossFactor, type(uint128).max - lastLossFactor) to receive more credit than their proportional share after a bad debt event, under preconditions: lossFactor slightly above lastLossFactor, credit is a small value causing mulDivDown to round up the survival ratio, violating invariant: sum of all lenders' postSlashCredit must not exceed totalUnits after bad debt realization, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency via credit inflation? Proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting sum(postSlashCredit for all lenders) <= marketState.totalUnits after each liquidation.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender exploit the rounding in postSlashPendingFee = pendingFee - pendingFee.mulDivUp(credit - postSlashCredit, credit) to retain more pendingFee than proportional after slashing, under preconditions: credit - postSlashCredit is small relative to credit, mulDivUp rounds up the slash amount reducing pendingFee by more than proportional, violating invariant: postSlashPendingFee / postSlashCredit must equal original pendingFee / credit ratio (within 1 wei), causing scoped impact: systematic pendingFee undercharge after bad debt, leaking continuous fee revenue? Proof idea: fuzz credit, pendingFee, lossFactor delta and assert postSlashPendingFee * credit <= pendingFee * postSlashCredit + credit.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: updatePositionView] Can an unprivileged lender exploit mulDivDown rounding in fee = postSlashPendingFee.mulDivDown(accrualEnd - lastAccrual, maturity - lastAccrual) to accrue zero fee for a nonzero time interval, under preconditions: postSlashPendingFee is small (e.g., 1), accrualEnd - lastAccrual < maturity - lastAccrual, causing mulDivDown to return 0, violating invariant: continuousFeeCredit must increase by the correct accrued fee amount, causing scoped impact: systematic continuous fee leakage for small credit positions? Proof idea: fuzz postSlashPendingFee in [1, 100] and time intervals and assert fee > 0 whenever postSlashPendingFee > 0 and accrualEnd > lastAccrual.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit mulDivDown rounding in lossFactor update: type(uint128).max - (type(uint128).max - _lossFactor).mulDivDown(_totalUnits - badDebt, _totalUnits) to set a lossFactor that is slightly higher than the mathematically correct value, under preconditions: badDebt is small relative to totalUnits, causing mulDivDown to round down the survival ratio and round up the lossFactor, violating invariant: lenders must not lose more than badDebt in aggregate, causing scoped impact: systematic over-slashing of lender credit beyond actual bad debt? Proof idea: invariant test asserting sum of all lenders' credit decrease equals badDebt within 1 wei per lender.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the mulDivDown rounding in continuousFeeCredit scaling: continuousFeeCredit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - newLossFactor, type(uint128).max - oldLossFactor) to cause continuousFeeCredit to decrease by more than the proportional bad debt share, under preconditions: multiple sequential bad debt events with small badDebt each time, violating invariant: continuousFeeCredit must remain claimable by feeClaimer proportional to surviving credit, causing scoped impact: systematic leakage of continuous fee credit to zero over many liquidations? Proof idea: fuzz sequence of N liquidations with small badDebt and assert continuousFeeCredit / totalUnits ratio is preserved within rounding tolerance.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit integer division truncation in the post-maturity LIF ramp: WAD + (_maxLif - WAD) * (block.timestamp - market.maturity) / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF to obtain lif = WAD (no incentive) for the first TIME_TO_MAX_LIF - 1 seconds after maturity when _maxLif - WAD < TIME_TO_MAX_LIF, under preconditions: market with LLTV near 1 (e.g., LLTV_8 = 1e18), maxLif = WAD (no incentive), block.timestamp = market.maturity + 1, violating invariant: post-maturity liquidation incentive must ramp linearly from 1 to maxLif, causing scoped impact: liquidator receives no incentive for first 14 minutes 59 seconds, potentially making liquidations unprofitable and leaving bad debt unrealized? Proof idea: unit test lif at block.timestamp = market.maturity + 1 and assert lif >= WAD.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the double mulDivUp in repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) to compute repaidUnits that is 2 wei higher than the true value, under preconditions: seizedAssets chosen so each mulDivUp rounds up by 1, violating invariant: repaidUnits must not exceed the fair value of seizedAssets at the oracle price divided by lif, causing scoped impact: liquidator overpays by up to 2 units per liquidation, or borrower's debt is reduced by more than the collateral value? Proof idea: differential test repaidUnits against exact rational arithmetic for all (seizedAssets, price, lif) combinations.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the double mulDivDown in seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, liquidatedCollatPrice) to receive fewer seized assets than the fair value of repaidUnits * lif / price, under preconditions: repaidUnits chosen so each mulDivDown rounds down by 1, violating invariant: liquidator must receive at least repaidUnits * lif / price collateral assets, causing scoped impact: liquidator receives 2 wei less collateral than entitled, making marginal liquidations unprofitable and leaving unhealthy positions unliquidated? Proof idea: fuzz repaidUnits and price and assert seizedAssets * price / lif >= repaidUnits - 2.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the WAD * WAD = 1e36 intermediate value in maxRepaid = (debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD * WAD, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) to cause an overflow or incorrect result when lif * lltv approaches WAD * WAD, under preconditions: lif = maxLif(LLTV_8, LIQUIDATION_CURSOR_HIGH) = WAD (since LLTV_8 = WAD), lltv = WAD, causing WAD * WAD - lif * lltv = 0 (division by zero), violating invariant: maxRepaid must be type(uint256).max when lltv = WAD, causing scoped impact: revert or incorrect RCF bypass for LLTV=1 markets? Proof idea: unit test liquidate with LLTV_8 and assert maxRepaid = type(uint256).max.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit mulDivUp rounding in maxRepaid = (debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD * WAD, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) to obtain a maxRepaid that is 1 unit higher than the true RCF cap, under preconditions: debt - maxDebt = 1, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv chosen to maximize rounding, violating invariant: repaidUnits must not exceed the amount needed to restore health plus rounding tolerance, causing scoped impact: liquidator can liquidate slightly more than the RCF allows, leaving borrower slightly healthier than intended but extracting extra collateral? Proof idea: fuzz (debt, maxDebt, lif, lltv) and assert post-liquidation health check.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the RCF bypass condition: collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, lif).zeroFloorSub(maxRepaid) < rcfThreshold to bypass the RCF by choosing rcfThreshold = 0 at market creation, under preconditions: market created with rcfThreshold = 0, any unhealthy position, violating invariant: RCF must prevent over-liquidation of positions that can be restored to health, causing scoped impact: full liquidation of positions that should only be partially liquidated, extracting excess collateral? Proof idea: create market with rcfThreshold=0 and assert full liquidation is always allowed.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: isHealthy / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the double mulDivDown in maxDebt += collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) to compute a maxDebt that is up to 2 wei lower than the true value per collateral, under preconditions: multi-collateral market with 16 active collaterals, each contributing 2 wei of rounding error, total maxDebt underestimated by up to 32 wei, violating invariant: healthy positions must not become liquidatable due to rounding, causing scoped impact: healthy borrower with debt exactly equal to true maxDebt can be liquidated due to 32-wei rounding error? Proof idea: fuzz 16-collateral position at exact health boundary and assert isHealthy returns true.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator exploit the double mulDivUp in badDebt computation: badDebt.zeroFloorSub(collateral.mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, maxLif)) to compute a badDebt that is 0 when the true bad debt is positive, under preconditions: collateral value slightly above debt when computed with mulDivUp, causing zeroFloorSub to return 0, violating invariant: bad debt must be realized when collateral value at maxLif is less than debt, causing scoped impact: bad debt not socialized among lenders, protocol insolvency? Proof idea: fuzz (debt, collateral, price, maxLif) near the bad debt boundary and assert badDebt > 0 when true collateral value / maxLif < debt.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: TickLib.tickToPrice] Can an unprivileged maker exploit the cubic Taylor series approximation error in wExp to create a tickToPrice value that differs from the true e^x by more than PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP = 1e12, under preconditions: tick near MAX_TICK = 5820 or tick = 0, where the approximation error is largest, violating invariant: tickToPrice must be monotonically non-decreasing and within 1e12 of the true exponential price, causing scoped impact: maker offers at ticks near extremes have mispriced spread, creating extractable arbitrage? Proof idea: differential test wExp against a high-precision reference for all ticks in [0, MAX_TICK] and assert |tickToPrice(tick) - true_price| <= PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: TickLib.tickToPrice] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the double rounding in tickToPrice: first divHalfDownUnchecked(1e36, 1e18 + wExp(...)) then divHalfDownUnchecked(result, PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP) * PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP to obtain a price that is up to 2 * PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP = 2e12 lower than the true price, under preconditions: any tick, violating invariant: tickToPrice must be within PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP of the true price, causing scoped impact: systematic underpricing of offers by up to 2e12 WAD units, creating extractable value for takers? Proof idea: fuzz all ticks and assert tickToPrice(tick) >= true_price - 2 * PRICE_ROUNDING_STEP.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: TickLib.wExp] Can an unprivileged actor exploit the unchecked arithmetic in wExp for the negative branch: 1e36 / wExp(-x) to obtain a result that is 1 wei lower than the true value due to integer division, under preconditions: x < 0 (tick > MAX_TICK/2 = 2910), causing the negative branch to compute 1e36 / wExp(-x) with truncation, violating invariant: wExp must be consistent between positive and negative branches (wExp(x) * wExp(-x) should equal 1e36), causing scoped impact: asymmetric pricing between buy and sell offers at the same tick, creating extractable spread? Proof idea: for all ticks > 2910, assert wExp(LN_ONE_PLUS_DELTA * (MAX_TICK/2 - tick)) * wExp(-LN_ONE_PLUS_DELTA * (MAX_TICK/2 - tick)) is within 1 of 1e36.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: TickLib.priceToTick] Can an unprivileged market creator exploit the ceiling rounding in priceToTick: (low + spacing - 1) / spacing * spacing to obtain a tick whose price is strictly higher than the input price, under preconditions: price is not exactly representable at a multiple of spacing, causing the returned tick to have tickToPrice > price, violating invariant: priceToTick must return the lowest tick multiple of spacing with price >= input price, causing scoped impact: offers placed at the returned tick have a higher price than intended, creating systematic overpricing? Proof idea: fuzz price and spacing and assert tickToPrice(priceToTick(price, spacing)) >= price.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the settlement fee spread asymmetry: for buy offers sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee and buyerPrice = offerPrice, while for sell offers sellerPrice = offerPrice and buyerPrice = offerPrice + settlementFee, to construct a sequence of buy-then-sell takes at the same tick that extracts value from the fee spread, under preconditions: market with nonzero settlementFee, same tick for both offers, violating invariant: round-trip buy+sell at same tick must not yield net positive assets for the taker, causing scoped impact: extractable value from fee spread asymmetry? Proof idea: simulate buy take then sell take at same tick and assert net asset flow is non-positive for taker.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the fact that for sell offers (offer.buy=false), buyerAssets and sellerAssets both use mulDivUp, so buyerAssets - sellerAssets = mulDivUp(units, buyerPrice, WAD) - mulDivUp(units, sellerPrice, WAD) which may equal 0 when units * settlementFee < WAD, under preconditions: sell offer, units=1, settlementFee < WAD, violating invariant: claimableSettlementFee must increase by at least 1 wei per take when settlementFee > 0, causing scoped impact: zero fee collection for sell offers with small units, systematic fee leakage? Proof idea: fuzz units in [1, WAD/settlementFee] for sell offers and assert buyerAssets > sellerAssets.

[File: 'src/libraries/EventsLib.sol -> Scope: High Rounding, fixed-point math, mulDiv, fee, spread, or time-to-maturity error that creates extractable value or systematic value leakage'] [Function: take] Can an unprivileged taker exploit the consumed tracking for maxAssets-based offers: consumed += buyerAssets (for buy offers) where buyerAssets = mulDivDown(units, buyerPrice, WAD), to fill more units than the maker intended by choosing units that minimize buyerAssets per unit, under preconditions: buy offer with maxAssets set, tick chosen so buyerPrice is just above a WAD multiple, causing mulDivDown to round down buyerAssets, violating invariant: total units filled must not exceed maxAssets / buyerPrice, causing scoped impact: offer overfill in unit terms beyond maker intent? Proof idea: fuzz units and tick for maxAssets offers and assert total units filled * buyerPrice / WAD <= maxAssets.
